page: Microsoft Azure E8-4as_v5 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the EASv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, which operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached Premium Storage for disk operations.

Performance benchmarks demonstrate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The server exhibits strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25 percent in stress-ng testing. Conversely, multi-core metrics, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores, fall into the bottom 25 percent. Memory bandwidth is average for large-block read operations but weak for write operations and small-block cached reads. Single-threaded decompression and text processing perform at average levels, while parallelized tasks and database operations show lower relative performance.

This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require a substantial memory footprint without demanding high multi-core CPU scaling. Because memory density is high relative to the vCPU count, users can run memory-bound applications without paying for unneeded compute cores. The server is best suited for memory-intensive applications, caching layers, single-threaded processing tasks, and lightweight web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure E8-4as_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EASv5 family, powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7763 processor clocked at 3.525 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per vCPU, though it lacks local storage and GPU resources. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution, but multi-core performance and memory write bandwidth are relatively weak. Qualitatively, this server offers high memory density for memory-bound workloads without the cost of excess CPU cores. It is best suited for memory-intensive applications, caching, and single-threaded tasks that benefit from a large memory footprint.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4as_v5 is an EASv5-family virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and no local storage, offering strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4as_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 4 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, designed for memory-bound, single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors for memory-bound workloads requiring strong single-core performance.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a high 16 GB per vCPU ratio
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
No local storage, utilizing network-attached Premium Storage
Best suited for memory-intensive, single-threaded applications and caching
